science.title: DNA robots could deliver drugs and hunt viruses inside your body
science.description: DNA robots are emerging as tiny programmable machines that could one day deliver drugs, hunt viruses, and build molecular-scale devices. By borrowing ideas from traditional robotics and combining them with DNA folding techniques, scientists are creating structures that can move and act with precision. These robots can be guided using chemical reactions or external signals like light and magnetic fields.

science.title: Artemis II launch: NASA prepares for Wednesday launch of historic space mission
science.description: Tuesday, March 31, 2026: Get the latest news and updates on the Artemis II mission as the countdown begins for NASA's return to the moon.

science.title: Are We About to Premanently Scar the Night Sky With One Million AI Satellite and 50,000 Space Mirrors?
science.description: If you thought the current crop of satellite megaconstellations was bad, you’re going to be horribly disappointed by new proposals from both SpaceX and a company called Reflect Orbital. Their combined plans would fundamentally alter the night sky as we know it, and the global astronomical community is sounding the alarm - most notably letters from the Royal Astronomical Society (RAS), the European Southern Observatory (ESO), and the International Astronomical Union (IAU) strongly opposing the plan, which currently sits with America’s Federal Communications Commission (FCC) for approval.

science.title: Photographer Jon McCormack captures nature's spinning, spiraling patterns
science.description: Jon McCormack's new book celebrates the whorls, hexagons, and half-moons that appear in the world all around us.

science.title: Scientists Just Found a Hidden Critical Point in Water Right Before It Freezes
science.description: As water gets colder, its behavior becomes increasingly weird from a physics perspective, and researchers looking for explanations have found something new: a previously hidden 'critical point' that emerges in supercooled water that doesn't freeze.

science.title: Airbus and ESA help NASA go back to the Moon
science.description: The European service module, built by Airbus for ESA and NASA is at the heart of the mission and is ready to power the historic Artemis II crew to the Moon

science.title: Into the fungal unknown: New tool maps fungal gene functions without reference genomes
science.description: While RNA sequencing (RNA-seq) has become a standard tool for profiling which genes are active in an organism, determining the actual biological functions of those genes in fungi remains a significant technical challenge. Most existing software tools are designed to cover a broad range of species, often lacking the specificity required for the unique genetic landscape of fungi. Furthermore, many non-model fungal species lack the high-quality reference genomes that traditional analysis methods rely on.

science.title: Massive x1.4 solar flare erupts just days before Artemis II launch: Here’s what NASA says
science.description: Science News: A powerful burst of solar activity briefly caught attention this week, arriving just days before one of the most closely watched space missions in rec.

ai.title: Blaize and Nokia Advance Collaboration with Joint AI Solution Showcase at GITEX Asia

(Nasdaq: BZAI, Nasdaq: BZAIW) ('Blaize'), a global leader in programmable, energy efficient AI computing, today announced the next phase of its collaboration with Nokia regarding joint validation and ecosystem engagement across Asia Pacific.

ai.title: AI-Powered Leap: Combating Pneumonia with Peptide Therapeutics
ai.description: A collaborative effort between India and Sweden at IIIT-Delhi is utilizing artificial intelligence for rapid drug discovery against pneumonia. The project leverages AI to design peptide-based therapeutics, offering a faster alternative to traditional methods. This innovation could help tackle drug resistance and revolutionize respiratory infection treatments.
